$meta.name: Rogue Films v2 design tokens
$meta.source: Figma file BuVndAwQ1zQKACDJFswneK — Rogue Website 2026 updates
$meta.notes: Figma file contains no native Variables or Styles — every value is inline. This token set is greenfield, inferred from observed usage across 7 desktop frames (canvas 0:1, 1728px) and 7 mobile frames (canvas 162:3727, 402px). Mobile values are Figma-sourced. Tablet values are proposed — no tablet canvas exists, so tablet values are linearly interpolated between mobile and desktop, rounded to clean numbers. Semantic tokens are resolved to static values here because the Token Bridge plugin does not support aliasing on JSON import; aliasing is preserved in Webflow via existing_variable_id. Names are usage-explicit (e.g. `page-title` not `display-1`). Motion easings stay in this JSON for Figma's sake (valid STRING variables in Figma) but are not created in Webflow — Webflow's Size variable type rejects `cubic-bezier()` values, so easings live as CSS custom properties in site custom code instead.

tokens.description: Brand navy. Primary page background on all non-Talent pages.
Brand accent pink. Active states, highlights, logo, headline block.
Brand cream. Primary text colour on dark surfaces.
Used as Talent-list surface base and for text on pink surface.
Reserved. Single Figma usage (film-poster caption).
Default page background. Resolves to navy.
Default body and heading text on dark background. Resolves to cream.
Highlighted / active text, active nav, active filter pill label, link colour. Resolves to pink.
Subtle borders on navy surfaces (pills, filters, search inputs). Cream at 30% alpha. Stored as rgba because alpha-on-reference isn't supported.
Display typeface. Used only for the 120px page H1s.
Body typeface. Nav, headings, body copy, labels.
Large page H1s (Talent, Joe Connor, Music Video, Film & TV, We are rogue). Mobile and Desktop are Figma-sourced; Tablet is interpolated.
Homepage pink statement block, News section heading. Mobile and Desktop Figma-sourced; Tablet interpolated.
Section heading (e.g. Contact 'Offices'). Mobile and Desktop Figma-sourced; Tablet interpolated.
Subsection heading (e.g. Contact city labels like 'London'). Mobile and Desktop Figma-sourced; Tablet interpolated.
Sub-subsection / lead paragraph (e.g. Contact intro). Mobile and Desktop Figma-sourced; Tablet interpolated.
Name/label role. Talent-list director names, Contact lead paragraph. 20px across all breakpoints per Figma.
Film thumbnail titles within grid overlays. Mobile 16px, Desktop 20px per Figma (equals `name` at desktop). Tablet interpolated.
Default body copy, nav links, filter pill labels, thumbnail director names, footer lines.
Footer copyright, news-grid titles, thumbnail director-name caption.
Regular weight.
Bold weight. Used on Px Grotesk thumbnail titles.
ExtraBold. Only Galano Grotesque headline usage.
Display sizes, page H1s, name-style lists. Unitless multiplier.
Standard body copy and reading contexts. Unitless multiplier.
Pill labels, nav links, footer address blocks. Unitless multiplier.
8px spacing. Used for tight gaps (e.g. filter-pill gap, thumbnail title-to-director gap).
10px spacing. Non-grid value — preserved for Figma fidelity.
15px spacing. Non-grid value — preserved for Figma fidelity.
16px spacing. Common pill padding / nav vertical padding.
20px spacing. Film thumbnail internal padding, mobile page h-padding.
24px spacing. Column row gaps.
32px spacing. Section inner gaps.
40px spacing. Default desktop page h-padding, major section gap.
80px spacing. Film-detail + Contact page h-padding.
120px spacing. Hero statement block padding.
Effectively fully rounded. All pill-shaped elements.
Standard mobile threshold.
Standard tablet threshold.
Webflow default desktop start.
Webflow default large-desktop start.
The only width Figma defines. Fluid scaling upper bound.
Quick micro-interactions (hover/focus states). Value in milliseconds.
Default transition (e.g. talent-page video crossfade). Value in milliseconds.
Slow/dramatic transitions. Value in milliseconds.
Standard ease. Figma-only token — defined in Webflow as a CSS custom property (--ease-standard) in site custom code, not a Webflow Variable.
Ease-in for elements leaving view. Figma-only token — see easing/standard note.
Ease-out for elements entering view. Figma-only token — see easing/standard note.
